mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate above 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals a weakening housing market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining housing starts below 1.1 million units are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production above 107 signals a strengthening supply chain that increases corporate profitability and reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising industrial production above 107 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in real disposable personal income below 4.4 trillion dollars signals a weakening consumer market that reduces corporate profitability and increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining real disposable personal income below 4.4 trillion dollars are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in initial unemployment claims above 1.4 million signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims above 1.4 million are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the consumer price index below 235 signals a weakening inflationary pressure that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with declining consumer price index below 235 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real GDP above 16.3 trillion dollars signals a strengthening demand market that increases corporate profitability and reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP above 16.3 trillion dollars are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
